tc10_evidence: tc10-fragility/probe.log — Maestro launched the app, connected to Metro, waited for chat-input, took screenshot; no render-surface freeze. The Sprint 02 fragility mode (freeze after any Maestro interaction following expo run:ios) does not reproduce. The Sprint 02 fix path (warm single-session Maestro runs against a pre-warmed simulator) works.

commits: 3274dc5e (over-fetch 4x on best-fallback for geometry filter headroom)
remediation_cycle_1.diagnosis_of_remaining_failure: The fix is logically correct (tested + typechecked) but STEP 2 still fails at runtime. Two hypotheses for the remaining blocker: (1) clearKeychain:true in launchApp resets the iOS TCC location permission, causing Location.requestForegroundPermissionsAsync() to show a native iOS permission dialog that Maestro cannot dismiss — location never resolves, and even with the fallback the dialog overlay may obscure pills; (2) the top-N rows by composite_score (even with 4x over-fetch = 20 rows) may not include routes with geometryStatus='generated' — the 19 generated routes in the 5,654-route catalog may be lower-ranked. Could not definitively verify either hypothesis because: (a) the Convex MCP has a stale cached project path bug (chdir to /Users/justinlich instead of /justinrich), blocking direct backend queries; (b) convex CLI requires authenticated identity for listCuratedRoutes; (c) the app environment after Maestro's clearState is finicky to inspect via Metro evaluate_js. This needs live-data verification against the authenticated Convex client.

step_results.evidence: App launched, dev-server picker shown, tapped localhost:8081 to connect, dev-menu popup dismissed, login screen reached. Screenshot 01-launch captured.
Tapped e2e-test-login-button; signed in via Clerk dev-keys against the test account e2e-login@jjrnshw9.mailosaur.net.

id:chat-input visible; id:route-on-map-marker NOT visible; id:route-line-on-map-marker NOT visible; literal text 'Discover' NOT visible. Screenshot 02-home-map-and-chat captured. Plan-view-as-landing seam (DISC-002) verified.

Tapped map-header-left-button → drawer opened. id:drawer-nav-settings visible; id:drawer-nav-saved visible; text 'Plan a ride' NOT visible; text 'Discover' NOT visible. Closed drawer. Text 'Filter', 'Sort', 'State' all NOT visible on home. Screenshots 03-drawer-only-settings-saved + 04-home-no-filter-sort-state captured.

extendedWaitUntil id:discovery-suggestion-pill-.* timed out at 30000ms in BOTH run 5 (sort fallback fix) and run 6 (sort fallback + over-fetch fix). CORRECTED DIAGNOSIS: the prior root cause claim ('BACKEND_DATA_INTEGRITY / geometryStatus field absent from schema') was WRONG — orchestrator verified via direct Convex REPL that the live deployment has 19 rows with geometryStatus='generated' and the field IS in the schema (convex/curatedRoutes.ts:57). Actual root cause was sort:'nearest' over-constraining discovery on cold boot; fixed with graceful fallback to sort:'best' + 4x over-fetch. STEP 2 STILL fails after the fix — suspected remaining cause is the clearKeychain:true + iOS location permission dialog interaction, OR the top-N-by-score rows lacking generated geometry even with over-fetch. See remediation_cycle_1.diagnosis_of_remaining_failure for detail.
Depends on STEP 2 succeeding. Flow halted at STEP 2 assertion failure.
